title: Reforms to Expand Foreign Participation in G-Secs: A Quick Update!
content:
# Reforms to Expand Foreign Participation in G-Secs: A Quick Update!

Ek important update hai. Government ne G-Secs mein foreign participation badhane aur capital markets ko deepen karne ke liye kai significant reforms announce kiye hain. These measures aim to attract more stable, long-term foreign capital into India.

Here’s a quick rundown of the key changes:

*   **Tax Exemption for FPIs:** Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) and Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) ko ab Government Securities (G-Secs) mein earned interest income aur capital gains (short-term and long-term) par income tax se poori tarah exempt kar diya gaya hai. Yeh exemption April 1, 2026, se hone wale income par applicable hoga. Iske liye Income-tax (Amendment) Ordinance, 2026, bhi insert kiya gaya hai.
*   **Fully Accessible Route (FAR) Expansion:** FAR framework ko ab expand kar diya gaya hai. Isme ab naye issuances of 15-year, 30-year, aur 40-year Government Securities ke saath-saath, FAR-eligible tenors mein issue kiye gaye Sovereign Green Bonds (SGrBs) bhi include honge.
*   **General Route Simplification:** FPIs ke liye General Route ke under investments ko simplify kiya gaya hai. Ab short-term investment limits, concentration limits, aur security-wise investment limits hata diye gaye hain. However, Central Government Securities ke liye 6% aur State Government Securities (SGSs) ke liye 2% ki overall quantitative investment limits remain unchanged. Additionally, 'general' aur 'long-term' investment limits ki sub-categories ko bhi ek single limit mein merge kar diya gaya hai.
*   **Liberalisation for Individual PROIs:** Is package mein, individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROIs) ko bhi ab listed Indian equities mein Portfolio Investment Scheme (PIS) ke through directly invest karne ki permission mil gayi hai, jo pehle sirf NRIs/OCIs ke liye available thi. Individual PROI ke liye single company mein investment limit 5% se badhaakar 10% kar di gayi hai, aur sabhi individual PROIs ke liye aggregate cap 10% se 24% kar diya gaya hai. Department of Economic Affairs (DEA) iske liye Foreign Exchange Management (Non-Debt Instruments) (Third Amendment) Rules, 2026, notify kar raha hai.

Ye reforms India ke bond markets ko deepen karne aur global investment destination ke roop mein India ki position ko strengthen karne mein madad karenge.
